I oversee operations for a pharmaceutical company.
My team handles deliveries across the country.
Right now, we are expanding into three new states.
This month, I am working on a new cold chain system.
So, Marcelo, we have talked about bikes all weekend, but I still don't know what you do back home. What do you do?
I oversee operations for a pharmaceutical logistics company. Basically, my team makes sure that medicine gets everywhere it needs to go, safely and on time.
That sounds simple, but I imagine it isn't. What is the hard part?
The cold chain. Medicine is time-sensitive, so we ensure it stays at the right temperature every hour. I liaise with suppliers, drivers and customs all day.
Fascinating. And are you working on anything new at the moment?
Yes, right now we are expanding into three new states, so I am building a whole new last-mile delivery team. It is the biggest project of my career.
My team makes sure medicine arrives safely and on time.
It sounds simple, but actually it is very complex.
What most people don't realize is how time-sensitive it is.

Hi Marcelo, it's Ingrid, from the cycling weekend. I really enjoyed hearing about what you do. It's funny, most people think logistics is just trucks and boxes, but the way you explained the cold chain for medicines really stayed with me. Listen, my brother runs a small food company here in Bergen, and right now he is looking for someone to talk to about how a supply chain actually works. I told him about you. No pressure at all, but if you have twenty minutes one evening, he would love to pick your brain over a video call. Let me know. And by the way, we are already planning next summer's ride. Take care.

Welcome back to the show. Today we are talking about the hidden world of logistics. Here is something most people don't realize: when you order medicine online, dozens of people and systems work together to get it to your door safely. A team plans the route, another team handles customs, and someone monitors the temperature every single hour. We call this the cold chain, and it never stops. Right now, as you listen to this, thousands of these deliveries are moving across the world. The challenge is simple to say and hard to do: the right product, in the right place, at the right temperature, at exactly the right time. It sounds simple, but actually it is one of the most complex jobs in the modern economy.
